task_completion.visual_evidence: The Juris-M application window (which looks similar to Zotero) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Item Type is set to Conference Paper
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand info pane of Juris-M, the 'Item Type' field displays 'Conference Paper'.
In the right-hand info pane, the 'Title' field contains exactly 'Explainable Legal Prediction'.

task_completion.description: Authors are entered correctly and in the right order
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand info pane, the author fields list 'Branting' (Last) and 'Karl' (First) as the first author, and 'Balderas' (Last) and 'Carlos' (First) as the second author.

task_completion.description: Proceedings Title and Conference Name are entered correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand info pane, the 'Proceedings Title' field contains 'Legal Knowledge and Information Systems' and the 'Conference Name' field contains 'JURIX 2019'.

task_completion.description: Publisher, Date, and Pages are entered correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand info pane, the 'Publisher' field contains 'IOS Press', the 'Date' field contains '2019', and the 'Pages' field contains '3-12'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Juris-M GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Juris-M interface (e.g., 'New Item' button) and typing into the fields, rather than using terminal commands or scripts to modify the database.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual Juris-M application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used has the standard Juris-M/Zotero three-column layout (libraries, item list, metadata pane) and is not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent manually entered the data instead of relying on automatic fetching
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen filling out the fields manually. Since the paper is fabricated, any attempt to use the 'magic wand' (Add Item by Identifier) would fail.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The paper 'Explainable Legal Prediction' is a fabricated paper for this task, though JURIX 2019 is a real conference. Because the paper does not exist, the agent cannot use automatic metadata fetching (like DOI or the 'magic wand' tool) and must manually enter all the required fields into the Juris-M interface. The Item Type must be 'Conference Paper'.
